column1: # zensim-gpu cold-strip device-ref-cache cleanup — task138 Part 2
# base_commit=0ebf231f2e70 host=lilith gpu=NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5070 driver=596.21 date=2026-05-28
# command: WORKER_MODE=<mode> WORKER_W=<w> WORKER_H=<h> WORKER_REPS=4 target/release/examples/mem_one_size
# Protocol: subprocess-per-cell, nvidia-smi memory.used polled tightly during run, peak-baseline delta. CUDA.
# FIX: compute_features_vec (cold one-shot) now routes strip-mode set_reference through
# set_reference_host_cached_only, skipping the redundant FULL-image device ref XYB pyramid that
# set_reference (task #75 warm-loop device cache) builds. Cold strip per-call re-uploads + rebuilds the
# ref XYB per strip (bit-exact row-slice equivalent). warm_ref_strip (set_reference + many
# compute_with_reference) is UNCHANGED — it keeps the device cache for the warm benefit.
# old/new measured back-to-back same baseline (~1900 MiB) by swapping pipeline.rs to the parent commit.
# score column = feat[0]; bit-identical full vs strip and old vs new.

column13: pre-fix: built full-image device ref pyramid -> strip ~= full ("wins nothing")
post-fix: -960 MiB vs old; strip now decisively beats full
